title: Of the Training of Black Men
venue: A. C. McClurg and Co., Chicago

license.note: Published 1903; public domain in the United States.

abstract: Of the Training of Black Men (1903), by W. E. B. Du Bois. From The Souls of Black Folk.
excerpt: Why, if the Soul can fling the Dust aside, And naked on the Air of Heaven ride, Were’t not a Shame—were’t not a Shame for him In this clay carcase crippled to abide? OMAR KHAYYÁM (FITZGERALD). [Illustration] From the shimmering swirl of waters where many,...
